sessions.transcript:
Quick recap
This meeting was an introductory session between Nelly (the EPQ supervisor) and Raymond (student) to discuss Raymond's EPQ project on "How to Enhance the Emotion of Brain Computer Interface Games." Raymond presented his project plan, which includes researching different types of brain-computer interfaces, designing a questionnaire, and exploring three methods to enhance game emotion: reducing delays, improving input methods, and incorporating AI elements. Nelly provided feedback on the project plan and explained the EPQ process, including the creation of a reading list with 7-10 scholarly articles and the completion of Form 2. They discussed the project timeline, with meetings scheduled through July, and set the next meeting for April 5th at 7 AM China time. Simon attended the conversation to observe Raymond's progress.

Next steps
zzr: Complete Form 2 (project proposal authorization) before the next meeting
zzr: Create a reading list with 7-10 scholarly articles related to the project topic, including summaries, and share in WeChat
zzr: Translate mind map into English and share in WeChat
Nelly: Send calendar with meeting schedule to zzr via WeChat
All: Attend next meeting on Sunday, 5th April at 7 AM China time

Summary

EPQ Project Advisor Introduction Meeting
Nelly introduced herself as the student advisor for Raymond's EPQ project and explained her background in science education. Raymond, a Grade AS student from Gonghua Cameron International School in Shanghai, presented his project topic on enhancing emotions in brain-computer interface games, which he plans to complete by late June or July. When asked about his career plans, Raymond expressed interest in biome engineering, and Nelly advised him to prepare a mind map for their next meeting.

EPQ Brain-Computer Interface Project Planning
Nelly and zzr discussed zzr's EPQ project plan focused on enhancing emotions in brain-computer interface games. zzr presented a three-part approach including selecting an interface type, designing a questionnaire to gather user feedback, and exploring three methods to increase emotion (reducing delays, improving input methods, and incorporating AI). Nelly encouraged zzr to be flexible with the plan and make adjustments based on new insights gained from further research, emphasizing that adaptability is an important part of the EPQ process.

Dissertation Project Interface Comparison
Nelly discussed a dissertation project focusing on comparing different interface types, basic construction improvements, and enhancing game immersion through visual elements. Nelly approved the project topic and advised creating a reading list of 7-10 scholarly articles related to the topic, including titles, links, and summaries of key information. The next steps involve completing Form 2, which includes documenting Nelly's feedback and any planned changes, and creating the initial reading list for discussion at the next meeting.

Project Timeline and Schedule Planning
Nelly discussed the project timeline, confirming that the final presentation and project completion are scheduled for July. She outlined a rough calendar with approximately three meetings planned for each month from April to July, with flexibility to adjust around zzr's school exams. Nelly explained that she would share a screenshot of the reading list example via WeChat for reference.

Dissertation Timeline and Schedule Adjustments
Nelly and Raymond discussed adjusting the session schedule, moving session 8 to June due to Raymond's scheduling conflict. They reviewed the timeline for completing dissertation tasks across April, May, June, and July, with specific deliverables assigned to each month. The conversation ended with scheduling their next meeting for Sunday, April 5th at 7 AM China time, and Nelly outlined two key tasks for Raymond to complete before then: finishing Form 2 and creating a reading list.

👤 学生：章哲睿（Raymond）｜ 📖 课题：如何提升脑机接口的沉浸感
⏱ 本次课时：45 分钟 ｜ 📊 剩余：8 / 9 SA 课时

📝 本次课程概要
本次课程为 EPQ 项目的首次学术督导会议，导师与学生完成了项目启动介绍及研究计划的深度沟通。双方确认了关于“脑机接口游戏沉浸感增强”的研究框架，包括问卷设计、延迟优化及 AI 元素引入等核心方法。会议详细讲解了 EPQ 学术规范流程，制定了从 4 月至 7 月的项目推进时间表，并安排了下次督导会议的具体时间。

✨ 学生表现与进展
Raymond 在会议中展示了清晰的研究思路和对生物工程领域的浓厚兴趣，能够主动阐述项目规划与职业愿景。面对导师的建议，他表现出良好的学术适应力，愿意根据深入调研的结果灵活调整研究方案。整体沟通顺畅，展现了作为 AS 阶段学生在独立研究项目上的专注度与潜力。

📋 课后任务
• 完成 Form 2（项目计划书授权表）的填写与提交，记录导师反馈及后续计划变更。
• 构建阅读清单，整理 7-10 篇与课题相关的学术文献，包含标题、链接及关键内容摘要，并通过微信分享。
• 将现有的项目思维导图翻译成英文版本，完成后发送给导师审核。

💬 导师寄语
科研探索始于扎实的资料梳理与开放的思维态度，期待在下一次会面中看到你更具深度的思考成果！

sessions.transcript:
Quick recap
Nelly and zzr met to discuss progress on zzr's EPQ (Extended Project Qualification) research. They reviewed Form 2 and Form 3 documentation, with Nelly providing guidance on creating a detailed mind map and explaining the structure of Form 4. zzr shared that they had read approximately 6-7 articles (mostly in Chinese) for their project combining computer games and physics knowledge, though they still needed to translate their mind map from Chinese to English. They discussed the importance of having a detailed mind map to help organize the project and set clear objectives. Nelly showed an example of a previous student's Form 4 to demonstrate how to properly structure the planning form, including specific activities, timelines, resources, and contingency plans. They scheduled their next meeting for April 19th at 10 AM after zzr's upcoming exams.


Next steps
zzr: Translate mind map to English and share in WeChat
zzr: Create reading list of articles/literatures and prepare to discuss in next meeting
zzr: Adjust and add more detail to mind map
Nelly: Complete Form 3 once reading list is received from zzr
Nelly: Post meeting notes in WeChat

Summary

EPQ Research Progress Discussion
Nelly and Raymond discussed Raymond's EPQ research progress, where he had read approximately 6-7 articles, mostly in Chinese with only 2 in English literature. Raymond expressed that his research topic was too broad and wanted to narrow it down to make it more focused. Nelly requested that Raymond share his reading list and discuss the key information from the articles he had read, though the conversation ended before this discussion could fully take place.

Mind Map Translation Task
Nelly instructed zzr to share the mind map in WeChat and translate it from Chinese to English as a task for the following week. Nelly emphasized the importance of creating a detailed mind map, using a previous student's example that included sections on disease mechanism, diagnosis, animal model investigation, therapy, and evaluation criteria. Nelly advised zzr to add more detailed sections to the existing mind map to make the project planning process clearer.

Task Organization and Form Process
Nelly instructed zzr to create a detailed mind map to help organize their tasks and encouraged taking screenshots for later reference. Nelly explained the process for Form 3, which involves reviewing a chosen topic after reading 7-10 articles and getting approval, and outlined that this form would include questions for zzr to answer or clarify.

Dissertation Project Planning Discussion
Nelly and zzr discussed the dissertation project, confirming it is not a group project and reviewing questions about the title's clarity and purpose. zzr explained their interest in combining computer games with physics knowledge, and Nelly noted zzr's future plans to major in biomedical engineering. They agreed to leave the section on research and resources incomplete until zzr creates a reading list and shares relevant articles in a future session.

Raymond's EPQ Project Planning Discussion
Nelly discussed Raymond's previous experience with EPQ lessons, where he learned about research methods and academic work. Nelly confirmed that Raymond's project is achievable within the proposed timeline and will have sufficient scope to meet all assessment objectives. The presentation date was left undetermined, with Nelly indicating it would be discussed closer to the end of the project.

Presentation Planning and Task Updates
Nelly discussed the presentation date, which remains TBC pending confirmation. She reviewed Form 3 and noted that Form 2 had been completed. Nelly requested that Raymond create a reading list for future discussion, though she acknowledged his current focus on mock exams might delay this task.

Form 4 Guidance Meeting Planning
Nelly and zzr scheduled their next meeting for Sunday, April 19th at 10 AM, ensuring zzr would have completed exams by then. Nelly discussed plans to guide zzr through completing Form 4 using a previous student's example, focusing on how to structure aims starting with "to investigate."

EPQ Form 4 Guidance Session
Nelly explained the structure and requirements of Form 4 for an EPQ project, covering aims, objectives, activities and timescale, resources, research methods, contingencies, and review dates. She provided detailed guidance on how to properly complete each section, emphasizing the importance of specific dates and clear planning. Nelly assigned two tasks for zzr to work on after their next meeting: translating the mind map to English and adding it to WeChat, and creating a reading list for discussion in their next meeting scheduled for April 19th at 10am.

sessions.title: SA #3
sessions.summary:
Quick recap

Nelly and Raymond met to discuss progress on Raymond's EPQ (Extended Project Qualification) focused on brain-computer interface games. They reviewed Raymond's completed mind map and reading list, with Nelly suggesting changes to the title from "enhance" to "increase" immersion. They worked through Form 4 of the project plan, defining objectives including defining and comparing invasive, semi-invasive, and non-invasive BCI methods, designing a questionnaire, creating a virtual world, and suggesting ways to improve games and BCI interfaces. Nelly explained the structure and requirements for the Gantt chart project management tool, showing examples from other students. They scheduled their next meeting for May 3rd at 10 AM to review Form 4 and the Gantt chart, with future meetings to be discussed after Raymond's exams end on June 3rd.

sessions.transcript:
Summary

Exam Schedule and EPQ Planning
Nelly and Raymond discussed his busy schedule due to upcoming exams starting April 30th and ending June 3rd. They agreed to pause their sessions during exam time and resume weekly meetings after exams finish, which would allow for approximately 8 meetings over about 8 weeks to complete the EPQ work.

Mind Map Translation Progress
Nelly and Raymond discussed the progress on translating a mind map into English, which Raymond planned to send via WeChat. They also mentioned creating a reading list, though no specific updates were provided on this task. Nelly reviewed the mind map together with Raymond and noted the inclusion of an item about staying passionate and bringing in interactive games.

BCI Game Immersion Enhancement Discussion
Nelly and Raymond discussed enhancing the immersion of brain-computer interface games, with Nelly suggesting changing the title from "enhance" to "increase" the immersion. They worked on sharing a reading list, with Nelly suggesting using a link instead of attaching the document directly. The conversation ended with Nelly asking Raymond about their favourite article from the reading list, but the response was not captured in the transcript.

BCI and VR Article Review
Nelly and Raymond discussed the review of articles related to BCI (Brain-Computer Interface) and VR, with Raymond highlighting three particularly useful articles covering the history of BCI games, famous figures in the field, and the combination of VR with BCI to enhance emotional aspects of games. Nelly advised focusing on more recent articles from the past five to seven years for the dissertation, while using older articles for historical context. Nelly confirmed that the Form 3 was ready to be submitted to the internal moderator and mentioned that the next step would involve working on the Form 4 project plan.

Form 4 Project Planning Discussion
Nelly and Raymond discussed the structure and content of a Form 4 project, focusing on defining aims and objectives related to enhancing immersion in brain-computer interface games. They reviewed a mind map to identify key objectives, including defining and comparing invasive, semi-invasive, and non-invasive brain-computer interfaces, discussing methods to increase immersion, and creating a virtual world application. Nelly emphasised the importance of starting with clear definitions and outlined plans to develop a questionnaire to gather insights on user preferences and immersion methods.

Dissertation Project Planning Discussion
Nelly and Raymond discussed the objectives for Raymond's dissertation project, which include defining and comparing topics, discussing methods to increase immersion, designing a questionnaire, creating a virtual world, and suggesting improvements for games and brain-computer interfaces. Nelly provided guidance on structuring the project plan, including how to document activities and timelines with specific start and end dates. The discussion focused on organising the work into manageable tasks with clear deadlines, starting from March 1st for topic selection and continuing through March 22nd for creating a mind map.

Project Planning and Form Guidance
Nelly guided Raymond through the project planning process, explaining how to complete Form 4, which includes activities and timescales. Nelly outlined key project components, including completing forms, writing the introduction and methodology, conducting literature reviews, and designing questionnaires, with specific activities scheduled for different timeframes, including discussion and evaluation around June. Nelly also explained the need to list resources required for the project, including databases like CNKI and Google Scholar.

Research Documentation Structure Guidelines
Nelly explained the structure of research documentation, distinguishing between primary research (including questionnaires) and secondary research (literature reviews). She clarified that advice from teachers doesn't need to be documented unless it involves interviewing teachers, in which case, specific details about the interview should be included. Nelly also outlined the contingency section, explaining it should describe alternative actions to take if challenges arise during the research process.

Project Management Tools Training Session
Nelly guided Raymond through completing Form 4 and creating a Gantt chart as project management tools. She explained how to structure both documents, showing examples from other students to illustrate the process. They scheduled their next meeting for May 3rd at 10 AM to review the completed Form 4 and Gantt chart, after which they will discuss the writing of the article and plan future meetings.
sessions.privateNotes:
作业：
- Complete form 4
- create a Gantt chart  

表2
- Nelly建议把题目里的 ”enhance” 改成 “increase”

Reading list
- 比较年久的文献还是建议只要作为历史背景来引用
- 不确定他对论文的理解怎么样

表4
- Nelly帮忙改了一下 Aim
- Objectives有些写的有点太雄心壮志了，”4. Create a virtual world”真的做得到吗
  - 哦是思维导图里的一部分，真正的这条objective应该是对虚拟世界的讨论，讨论BCI技术的成熟与应用对于虚拟实际的建立意味着什么
  - 现在表里的objectives是Nelly在课上按照思维导图的内容填的，我得跟哲睿聊一聊，好好细化一下objectives，这个太重要了
  - 可以跟他再做一次思维导图，这一次把整个论文的结构设计出来，包括论证方法和论证思路。其实他的沟通记录文档里有一个我给他做的英文思维导图，估计是忘了…

- 同样教他怎么做

表6
- 现在还完全没填啊，得让他开始填了

时间安排：
- 说明了希望下下次meeting在6月3日之后

2026.04.25 TA Meeting 会议记录

sessions.homework:
1.表4填写
学生用书p.202，范文p.20
重要：填写Aim，Objectives & Activities：
●填写方式参考：https://doc.weixin.qq.com/doc/w3_ASIAgwY1ADoCN7bHzEcQVQ7OBrhVZ?scode=AEQAlwdCAAoMSDWNJlASIAgwY1ADo 一定要认真填写， AO1分数关联最大的部分，尤其是objectives是否全部完成。

目前的Aim：
To investigate what is the better way at enhancing immersion of brain-computer interface games by knowing why people would be immersed in the games and how to improve the games and the brain-computer interfaces.

1.Define and compare invasive, semi-invasive and non-invasive 把句子补全，然后修改一下标点和空格，包括下面提到的"immersion"也还没有定义，记得加进去
2.Discuss methods to increase immersion of brain computer interfaces 哪些methods？自己发明的还是现有的？
3. will design a questionnaire to find out how people feel to the brain-computer interfaces and what is more important about the games for people to immerse in.
3.I will design a questionnaire to find out people's awareness (有多少人了解脑机接口) and perspectives (是否能接受) about the brain-computer interfaces and what are the more important factors of a game for people to immersed in.     改一下句式
4.Create a virtual world 建立虚拟世界应该不在你的计划里，这一条要修改或者直接去掉
a.实际上应该为“讨论脑机接口如何能够更好帮助接入虚拟世界”
5.Suggest ways to improve the games and the brain-computer interfaces. Be specific, 基于什么给出这些suggestions?  -- 基于你自己的研究。同时也重新措一下辞。
●Give suggestions on how can BCI games improve the immersiveness for the players base on my findings.

Resources, Research, Contingencies：
●按照学生用书上的解读填写即可
●Resources：
○文献数据库
○调查问卷的网站（问卷星），以及发放群体，在哪里发放，以及简短的解释
●Research：
○要进行的研究
○文献综述
○对调查问卷结果进行分析
○结合调查问卷结果以及文献学习，得出结论(give suggestions...)
●Contingencies:
○考试：复习时间增加，项目时间可能受影响 -- 将部分计划延后，在考试结束后catch up
○问卷数量不够：发放到多个平台，并且尽快开始，在考试导致项目中断之前就发送问卷


Reviews：
●项目规划（AO1）很重要的一部分，每一个review都需要有日期和计划在该日期之前完成反思的内容
1.Initial review：
a.反思：我的选题是否有足够的资料收集切能够进行深入的研究
b.日期可以就填这几天，因为填写表4本身也是一个阶段性总结
2.Midterm review：
a.反思：我是否收集到了足够的数据？我是否要开始我的写作？我是否有了写作的思路？
3.Final review：
a.反思：我是否已经得出了结论并通过写作的方式很好的呈现出来了？我的论文可读性如何？逻辑是否清晰？

2.制作甘特图
学生用书p.61
确保与表4的Activities对齐，把关键的计划放进去即可
●建议：甘特图不仅仅是最终提交的有力证据，也是很好的项目管理工具。可以把自己其它的安排：考试，复习，其它已经确定了的安排也写进去，方便自己做时间规划管理。
●模板不好用的话可以找TA老师要工具


3.开始填写表6
活动日志，应该当成日记来写，你做了什么都要往里记录。同时你的表6需要参照表4（项目计划）来写，可以比表4更详细，因为我们是按照计划去实施的。

表6中需要注意的一个是comments部分，一个是时间部分。
●Comments部分需要好好的去写。可以评价自己完成的情况，你的反思、收获等等（AO4评分证据）。
●表6里可以有unplanned的内容，这个时候需要你在comments里去反思，为什么加上了这个步骤，以及做的情况，是否解决了问题等。
●时间部分需要注意的是，所有的项目时间加起来要大于120小时，所以建议时间可以多写一点。比如写作阶段可以按天写
●表6的条目需要大于20条。

4.规划设计问卷调查问题
问卷调查网站：问卷星。
●先写下打算提出的问题，然后进行微调修改。
●问题写好后先给TA老师看，然后在SA会议上与SA老师讨论


5.思维导图画出论文结构
根据自己时间安排看有没有时间做，优先级较低，可以在考试期间碎片时间开始搭建


6.表2填写需要补充
学生用书p.200，范文p.18

📋 TA Meeting · 2026-04-25
👤 学生：章哲睿（Raymond） ｜ 📖 课题：BCI 沉浸感增强研究
⏱ 本次课时：50 分钟 ｜ 📊 剩余：6 / 9 SA 课时

📝 本次课程概要
本次课程重点聚焦于项目规划与执行标准的落实。导师详细指导学生优化了表 4 中的研究目标（Objectives）及活动计划，确保与 AO1 评分标准紧密对应。同时，就甘特图的时间管理、活动日志（表 6）的记录规范以及问卷调查的设计思路进行了系统讲解，并明确了后续的风险预案与复习资料安排。

✨ 学生表现与进展
Raymond 在本节课中展现了积极的学习态度和严谨的思维逻辑。在梳理研究目的时，能够认真听取反馈并对复杂句式进行调整，显示出对学术规范的重视。对于如何界定“沉浸感”及选择具体的研究方法，学生已有初步的思考框架，若能按计划完成资料收集与问卷设计，将为论文写作奠定坚实的数据基础。

📋 课后任务
• 完善表 4（Table 4）：重点关注 Aim 及 Objectives 的修改，补充关键术语定义（如 invasive/non-invasive），参考教材 p.202 及范文 p.20 进行句式润色，确保涵盖所有研究计划。
• 制作甘特图（Gantt Chart）：依据表 4 的计划对齐时间安排，建议将考试复习等个人重要日程一并纳入，以便更好地管理项目进度。
• 开始填写表 6（Activity Log）：作为活动日记需详细记录每日进度，总时长建议覆盖 120 小时以上，条目不少于 20 条，注重 Comments 部分的反思与收获。
• 规划设计问卷调查：利用问卷星等平台草拟问题，先自行微调后提交给 TA 审核，再与 SA 老师讨论确认。
• 绘制论文结构思维导图：可在考试期间的碎片时间进行搭建，辅助梳理论文逻辑。
• 补充表 2 内容：参照学生用书 p.200 及范文 p.18 补充相关信息。
